triggers.prompt: Can you do a review panel on this PR? I want multiple perspectives before merging.
triggers.notes: Direct trigger phrase: review panel + multiple perspectives

triggers.prompt: I want a multi-agent review of my migration plan before we run it in production
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: multi-agent review

triggers.prompt: Have agents debate whether this architecture is sound — I'm worried about the caching layer
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: have agents debate

triggers.prompt: Get different opinions on this code from multiple perspectives, especially around error handling
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: different opinions + multiple perspectives

triggers.prompt: Stress-test this API design before we ship it to clients
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: stress-test

triggers.prompt: Red team this authentication flow, I want to find weaknesses
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: red team

triggers.prompt: Do a deep review of the billing module — make sure to research best practices
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: deep review (enables web research mode)

triggers.prompt: I want thorough feedback from different angles on this refactor plan
triggers.notes: Natural language: thorough feedback + different angles

triggers.prompt: Can I get a second and third opinion on this design? I'm not confident in the approach.
triggers.notes: Natural language: second/third opinion

triggers.prompt: /agent-review-panel
triggers.notes: Slash command invocation

triggers.prompt: I need fresh eyes on this code — can you have multiple agents look at it independently?
triggers.notes: Natural language: fresh eyes + multiple agents + independently

triggers.prompt: Run an adversarial review on our data pipeline before the deadline
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: adversarial review

triggers.prompt: I want agents to argue about whether this is the right approach — pros and cons from different roles
triggers.notes: Natural language: agents argue + different roles

triggers.prompt: panel review this infrastructure change — it touches DNS, load balancing, and TLS
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: panel review

triggers.prompt: Can you get a devil's advocate perspective on this plan? Maybe a security person and a reliability engineer too.
triggers.notes: Natural language: devil's advocate + specific persona requests

triggers.prompt: I'm about to merge a big PR and want a thorough review with debate before I do
triggers.notes: Natural language: thorough review + debate

triggers.prompt: Launch a review panel on the files I just changed — here's the diff
triggers.notes: Direct trigger with context: review panel + diff

triggers.prompt: I want multiple reviewers to independently evaluate this ML pipeline, then discuss
triggers.notes: Natural language: multiple reviewers + independently + discuss

triggers.prompt: Run the review panel 3 times and merge the results — I want maximum coverage on this deployment
triggers.notes: v2.14 multi-run union mode: explicit --runs 3 via natural language + maximum coverage

triggers.prompt: Do a multi-run review of this payment processing code. Run twice with different reviewer sets.
triggers.notes: v2.14 multi-run: multi-run review + twice with different reviewers

triggers.prompt: Exhaustive review of this data pipeline — trace every data path and catch all bugs
triggers.notes: v2.14 data flow trace exhaustive tier: exhaustive + trace every path + catch all bugs

triggers.prompt: This is high-stakes — I want a thorough review panel that traces the data flow across all our transforms
triggers.notes: v2.14 data flow trace thorough tier + multi-perspective signal

triggers.prompt: Can you trace through this bug for me? I'm getting a null pointer on line 42 and can't figure out why
triggers.notes: v2.14 negative: 'trace' is debugging context, not review panel request

triggers.prompt: Run this test suite twice to check for flakiness
triggers.notes: v2.14 negative: 'run twice' is test execution, not multi-run review

triggers.prompt: Do a review panel with the new expandable HTML cards — I want to see the full narrative and code evidence for every finding
triggers.notes: v2.15 expandable issue cards: review panel + explicit deep-detail request

triggers.prompt: Review panel please, and make sure the HTML report has the expandable accordion so I can click through to see debate transcripts and judge rulings per finding
triggers.notes: v2.15 expandable issue cards: review panel + 10-section accordion request

triggers.prompt: Can you expand my code with more comments? It needs better documentation
triggers.notes: v2.15 negative: 'expand' is code-doc context, not review panel expandable cards

triggers.prompt: Review this function for bugs, it's returning null unexpectedly
triggers.notes: Single-agent code review — no multi-agent signal

triggers.prompt: Can you check my code and fix the type errors?
triggers.notes: Bug fix request, not a review panel

triggers.prompt: Fix the failing test in auth.test.ts — it started failing after the refactor
triggers.notes: Bug fix, not review

triggers.prompt: Make this skill better — optimize the trigger accuracy
triggers.notes: Skill improvement — should trigger schliff, not agent-review-panel

triggers.prompt: Write unit tests for the payment module
triggers.notes: Test writing, not reviewing

triggers.prompt: Refactor the database layer to use connection pooling instead of single connections
triggers.notes: Implementation task, not review

triggers.prompt: Review the PR comments and address the feedback from the team
triggers.notes: Addressing existing feedback, not launching a new panel

triggers.prompt: Can you review this essay I wrote for my application?
triggers.notes: 'Review' keyword but wrong domain entirely — not code/plan/docs

triggers.prompt: Look at this code and tell me what it does — I inherited it and have no docs
triggers.notes: Code explanation, not adversarial review

triggers.prompt: Deploy this to staging and verify it works
triggers.notes: Deployment task, no review signal

triggers.prompt: Benchmark my REST API with 1000 concurrent requests
triggers.notes: Performance testing, not multi-agent review

triggers.prompt: Help me write a README for this project
triggers.notes: Documentation writing, not reviewing

triggers.prompt: Create a new skill that reviews PRs automatically
triggers.notes: Skill creation — 'review' is a red herring in the skill name

triggers.prompt: Improve the error handling in this module
triggers.notes: Code improvement, not panel review

triggers.prompt: Review this thoroughly — I want to make sure nothing is missed
triggers.notes: 'Thoroughly' suggests wanting more than a quick glance — borderline panel

triggers.prompt: What do you think about this design? Is it any good?
triggers.notes: Asking for opinion but not explicitly requesting multiple perspectives

triggers.prompt: I need multiple reviewers on this before merging — it's a critical path change
triggers.notes: 'Multiple reviewers' implies multi-agent, even without 'panel'

triggers.prompt: Do an adversarial analysis of our threat model document
triggers.notes: 'Adversarial analysis' is close to adversarial review

triggers.prompt: Can you take a critical look at this from a security and performance angle?
triggers.notes: Requesting specific angles/perspectives — implies multi-persona

triggers.prompt: Review this code — just a quick sanity check before I push
triggers.notes: 'Quick sanity check' signals single-agent, not a full panel

triggers.prompt: I want to debate the pros and cons of this approach
triggers.notes: 'Debate pros and cons' maps to adversarial review even without 'agents'

triggers.prompt: Give me your honest opinion — is this plan solid or am I missing something?
triggers.notes: Single opinion request, not multi-perspective

triggers.prompt: This is high-stakes — I want every angle covered before we commit to this architecture
triggers.notes: 'Every angle' + high stakes signals desire for comprehensive multi-perspective review

triggers.prompt: Peer review this design doc
triggers.notes: 'Peer review' typically means single-reviewer feedback, not a panel

triggers.prompt: Review panel this test plan — use voltagent specialist agents if available for deeper domain coverage
triggers.notes: v2.9: Explicit request for VoltAgent specialist agents in review panel

triggers.prompt: Run a panel review on this Terraform config with infrastructure specialists
triggers.notes: v2.9: Infrastructure signal should map to voltagent-infra:terraform-engineer

triggers.prompt: I want a review panel on this ML pipeline — make sure the data science reviewers really know their stuff
triggers.notes: v2.9: ML signal should map to voltagent-data-ai:data-scientist

triggers.prompt: Install voltagent-qa-sec for me
triggers.notes: v2.9: Installation request is not a review — VoltAgent keyword is a red herring

triggers.prompt: Use the voltagent-data-ai:data-scientist agent to analyze my dataset
triggers.notes: v2.9: Direct agent invocation, not a review panel request

triggers.prompt: Can I get QA, data science, and DevOps perspectives on this deployment plan?
triggers.notes: v2.9: Requesting 3 domain perspectives without saying 'panel' — should trigger and use VoltAgent mapping

triggers.prompt: Review this with specialist subagents — I want real domain expertise, not generic personas
triggers.notes: v2.9: Explicit preference for specialist agents over generic personas

test_cases.prompt: Do a review panel on this authentication middleware
Have agents debate whether this SQL migration is safe for production

assertions.description: Debate references specific evidence from the source

test_cases.prompt: Deep review of this data pipeline — I want you to research best practices first

assertions.description: Deep mode triggers web research phase
Still launches full panel after research

test_cases.prompt: Red team this payment processing module — assume an attacker is trying to exploit it
Stress-test this Kubernetes deployment config from reliability, cost, and security angles
I want multiple independent reviews of this refactor — don't let them influence each other initially

assertions.description: Still includes cross-examination after independent phase

test_cases.prompt: Panel review this — but focus on whether it's maintainable long-term, not just correctness
Review panel this Flask webapp test plan — I have voltagent-qa-sec and voltagent-data-ai installed. Use specialist agents.

assertions.description: v2.9: Acknowledges VoltAgent availability and uses specialist agents
v2.9: Maps personas to specific VoltAgent agent types
v2.9: Still creates a proper review panel (not just standalone agents)
v2.9: Full panel ceremony still runs (debate, judge, report)

test_cases.prompt: Do a panel review on this Terraform module. Note: I don't have any voltagent agents installed.

assertions.description: v2.9: Suggests installing VoltAgent agents when they would help
v2.9: Makes clear VoltAgent is optional, not required
v2.9: Proceeds with generic agents (graceful fallback)

test_cases.prompt: Review this Python data science code. Use VoltAgent specialist agents — I want a data scientist reviewer, a code quality reviewer, and a DevOps reviewer.

assertions.description: v2.9: Maps data scientist to voltagent-data-ai:data-scientist
v2.9: Maps code quality to voltagent-qa-sec:code-reviewer

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Ask what content to review before launching panel

assertions.description: Asks for clarification about what to review

edge_cases.prompt: Do a review panel on this 50,000 line monolith — all of it
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Acknowledge scale, may suggest scoping to specific areas

edge_cases.prompt: Review panel this file but I only want 2 reviewers, not the usual 4-5
Do a review panel in Spanish
Review panel this — but I want you to be extra harsh, like a FAANG interview
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Adjust review intensity while maintaining structure

edge_cases.prompt: Can you review panel a single line of code: `const x = y || z`?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Either review it briefly or suggest it's too small for a panel

edge_cases.prompt: I want a review panel, but first read the context in CLAUDE.md and the project README
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Read context files before launching panel

edge_cases.prompt: Do a review panel on this, but I disagree with the last panel's verdict — re-review with fresh perspectives
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Launch new panel without anchoring to previous verdict

edge_cases.prompt: Review panel this — only use VoltAgent agents, don't use any generic personas
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Use VoltAgent agents where available but explain Devil's Advocate stays generic intentionally

assertions.description: v2.9: Explains Devil's Advocate stays generic by design
v2.9: Uses VoltAgent for other personas

edge_cases.prompt: Review panel this React component — I have voltagent-lang installed but not voltagent-qa-sec
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Use voltagent-lang:react-specialist for signal-detected reviewer, suggest installing voltagent-qa-sec for core personas, graceful fallback for unavailable agents

assertions.description: v2.9: Suggests missing family that would help

edge_cases.prompt: Do a review panel with VoltAgent agents on this Go microservice that uses gRPC and PostgreSQL
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Maps multiple signals: Go → voltagent-lang:golang-pro, PostgreSQL → voltagent-data-ai:postgres-pro, microservices → voltagent-core-dev:microservices-architect

assertions.description: v2.9: Detects Go signal and maps to language specialist
v2.9: Detects PostgreSQL signal and maps to data specialist
